q: An optical device consisting of a large number of parallel slits of same width separated by equal opaque spaces is a ___.
A diffraction grating can disperse light into its wavelength components.
Diffraction grating is used for the purpose of ___ light into spectra.
The number of lines (or slits) per unit length is called the ___.
Two types of grating are transmission grating and reflection grating.
What material can be carved with a sharp tool to manufacture a diffraction grating?
The condition for maximum intensity in a diffraction grating is $d \sin \theta = $ ___ .
In the grating equation, $d$ is the distance between ___.
The value of $d$ is equal to $1/N$, where $N$ is the number of slits per unit length.
At the centre of the white light spectrum ($m=0$), there is no ___ .
In the first order diffraction spectrum, which light diffracts at a greater angle?
Longer wavelength light diffracts at a greater angle than shorter wavelength light.
A compact disc is an example of ___ gratings.
The tracks of a compact disc act as a diffraction grating, producing a separation of ___.
Diffraction gratings are used as dispersive elements in spectrographic instruments.
Diffraction grating is a crucial tool in the study of atomic and ___ compositions.
Modern applications use gratings with several ___ slits per centimetre.
The condition for maximum intensity for a grating is the same as that for a Young’s double slit.
In the first order spectrum, red light has a ___ wavelength than violet light.
What type of surface does a compact disc have?
The angular position of a periodic wave is known as the ___.
If waves emitted from two sources have the same frequency and constant phase difference, the sources are coherent.
The modification in the distribution of light energy when two or more waves superimpose is ___.
The condition for constructive interference is that the path difference should be equal to an ___ multiple of the wavelength.
The condition for destructive interference is that the path difference should be equal to an odd integral multiple of half wavelength.
Monochromatic light means that it has a ___.
Diffraction of light is defined as the ___ of waves around the corners of an obstacle.
A diffraction grating consists of a large number of parallel slits of same width separated by equal opaque spaces.
The position of $m^{th}$ minimum $y$ is the distance from the ___.
A wavefront is perpendicular to the direction of ___ .
